name: notion-api
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "search Notion", "find in Notion", "search my Notion workspace", "create Notion page", "make a Notion page", "update Notion page", "edit Notion page", "query Notion database", "get Notion database", "read Notion page", "get page content from Notion", "list Notion pages", or mentions Notion integration, Notion workspace, or Notion API access.
capabilities: Search pages and databases across your Notion workspace
Get, create, and update Notion pages with properties and icons
Query databases with filters, sorting, and pagination
Read page content as structured blocks with nested children
author: BLTGV
securitySummary: All 240 static findings are false positives. The analyzer misidentified markdown code formatting and JavaScript template literals as shell command execution. No actual command injection, crypto usage, or malicious patterns exist. This is a legitimate Notion API wrapper that makes HTTP requests to api.notion.com using environment-based authentication.
valueStatement: Notion workspaces contain valuable documentation, tasks, and project data. This skill provides a TypeScript interface to search, read, and manage Notion content through the official API. Enable AI assistants to retrieve information from your knowledge base and create structured updates.
userTitle: Access Notion pages, databases, and content via API
limitations: Requires NOTION_TOKEN environment variable with Notion integration setup
Pages and databases must be shared with the integration to be accessible
Rate limited to approximately 3 requests per second by Notion API
Cannot create or modify block content within pages directly

useCases.title: Search team documentation
useCases.description: Find meeting notes, project specs, and reference docs stored in Notion

useCases.title: Query task databases
useCases.description: Retrieve and filter task lists with status, priority, and due date criteria

useCases.title: Automate Notion updates
useCases.description: Create pages and entries from CI/CD pipelines or automated workflows

promptTemplates.title: Database query
promptTemplates.prompt: Query the Notion database {id} for entries where Status equals "Done"

promptTemplates.title: Page creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Notion page under {parent_id} with title "{title}"

promptTemplates.title: Content retrieval
promptTemplates.prompt: List all blocks from Notion page {id} recursively

bestPractices: Set up a dedicated Notion integration with minimal required capabilities
Share only necessary pages and databases with the integration
Implement error handling for auth_required responses from the skill
Respect Notion API rate limits by batching requests when possible
antiPatterns: Storing NOTION_TOKEN in source code or version control
Sharing the integration with entire workspaces unnecessarily
Making excessive API calls without pagination or rate limiting
Ignoring error responses from the Notion API

faq.question: How do I set up the Notion integration?
faq.answer: Go to notion.so/my-integrations, create a new integration, copy the secret token, and set NOTION_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: Why am I getting access denied errors?
faq.answer: Pages and databases must be shared with your integration. Open them in Notion, click the menu, and add your integration as a connection.

faq.question: Can this skill write content to pages?
faq.answer: This skill can create pages and update page properties but cannot modify block content within existing pages.

faq.question: How many requests can I make?
faq.answer: Notion API allows approximately 3 requests per second on average. The skill returns errors when rate limited.

faq.question: What formats are supported for filters?
faq.answer: Filters use Notion's filter syntax with JSON objects for properties, checkboxes, dates, select fields, and compound conditions.

faq.question: Can I search both pages and databases?
faq.answer: Yes, the search function defaults to both. Use the filter option to limit results to pages or databases only.
